name: Kos Lemon Pie
recipeIngredient: 8 tbsp Unsalted butter (cut into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the pastry, sift the flour and confectioners' sugar into a large cold bowl. Stir in the grated lemon zest and salt. Add the butter and rub mixture together with your fingers until it resembles coarse meal.
2. Beat the egg yolks and vanilla together and stir into the flour mixture. Form dough into a ball, adding cold water only if necessary for softness. Cover tightly and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
3. Set rack in center of oven and heat to 375°F (190°C).
4. Lightly flour work surface and roll out pastry to fit 9-10 inch tart pan with removable bottom. Transfer pastry wrapped around rolling pin, lift above pan, and unroll over rim. Press pastry into pan, then use rolling pin to trim edges. Line with aluminum foil.
5. Bake for 6-8 minutes or until just set. Set aside and lower oven temperature to 325°F (160°C).
6. To make filling, beat butter and honey until pale and creamy. Beat in egg yolks one at a time. Stir in almonds, juice of 2 lemons (reserve zest, removed in thin strips), and cream.
7. Pour into pastry shell and smooth top with spatula. Bake for 30-40 minutes or until golden brown and set.
8. Meanwhile, combine superfine sugar, reserved lemon zest, and water in a saucepan. Bring to boil, then simmer 6 minutes or until syrup lightly coats back of metal spoon. Set aside to cool slightly.
9. Add remaining lemon juice to barely warm syrup and strain, reserving zest. Pour syrup over warm pie, remove from pan, and sprinkle generously with confectioners' sugar and reserved zest. Serve immediately.
Note: To make pastry in food processor, chill bowl and blade for 1 hour. Process flour, sugar, lemon zest, and butter until mixture resembles coarse meal. Add egg yolks and vanilla, pulsing just to mix. Cover and refrigerate.
